fields.definition_origin: Amos Tversky & Daniel Kahneman (1974) — 'Judgment under Uncertainty: Heuristics and Biases' (Science, vol 185, pp 1124-1131). Anchoring-and-adjustment heuristic: an initial value (the anchor) disproportionately weights all subsequent estimates, even when the anchor is arbitrary. Applied to first-impression interface perception by NN/g and confirmed in Lindgaard et al. (2006) 'Attention web designers: You have 50 milliseconds to make a good first impression' (Behaviour & Information Technology). Onboarding-specific replication in Sandström et al. anchor study (PMC12956955, three-replication cohort N1=100, N2=87, N3=80) showing initial complexity perception persists across the entire app utility judgment.
fields.core_mechanism: Cognition is sticky on first inputs. The user's appraisal of the first ~50ms of visual exposure (Lindgaard) and first ~7-10s of interactive content (PMC12956955) becomes the anchor that subsequent screens are *adjusted from*, not independently re-evaluated. If the opening reads 'complex', every later screen is rated more complex even when objectively simpler; if the opening reads 'caring', every later friction is partially absorbed. Mechanism is dual: (1) System-1 affective tagging (Zajonc 1980 'preferences need no inferences') runs faster than analytic evaluation, locking emotional valence; (2) memory consolidation prefers the anchor as a search-shortcut (Tversky-Kahneman adjustment is insufficient — users adjust from the anchor but rarely move far). Onboarding implication: the first 7-10s of the first screen are the most leveraged real estate in the entire app — they set the prior for every later interaction including the paywall.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Affective primacy is mediated by amygdala-driven rapid valence tagging (LeDoux 1996; Pessoa 2008) which runs ahead of cortical analysis. Anchoring effects in numeric tasks engage right inferior parietal cortex and dorsolateral PFC under fMRI (Qu et al. 2008). First-impression aesthetic judgments correlate with medial orbitofrontal cortex activation (Kawabata & Zeki 2004). Hippocampal binding consolidates the first-encounter context as the retrieval template for later encounters.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Tversky & Kahneman (1974) original anchoring papers — thousands of replications (e.g. Strack & Mussweiler 1997 selective accessibility model, n>200 each). (2) Lindgaard et al. (2006) — n=22 prototype + n=31 follow-up: aesthetic judgments form within 50ms and predict satisfaction ratings hours later (r ≈ 0.49). (3) Lindgaard, Dudek, Sen, Sumegi & Noonan (2011) — affective evaluations and anchoring across web pages, replication confirms first 500ms drives lasting judgment. (4) PMC12956955 (cited in Kleeve research base) anchor study, three replications N1=100, N2=87, N3=80 — initial onboarding complexity perception persists regardless of subsequent utility; effect significant across all three samples. (5) Bar (2004) 'Visual objects in context' — the brain's first-pass categorization is gist-level and primes all later perception. (6) Industry data: Adapty 2026 finds onboarding paywall converts at 1.35% (highest of any placement) — meaning the first session's Hook anchor sets paywall conversion before the paywall is even shown. (7) Mau Baron's Prayer Lock teardown: shifting the first 3 onboarding screens to mirror the TikTok ad creative semantically (UGC-onboarding alignment) tripled organic conversion.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: First-screen time-to-action (target <7s), first-screen abandonment %, screen-1-to-screen-2 conversion, perceived-complexity Likert immediately after first screen, NPS at end of onboarding regressed against first-screen sentiment, install-to-activation conversion.
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Lindgaard 2006: aesthetic judgments at 50ms correlate r ≈ 0.49 with later satisfaction ratings — single strongest variable in the regression. Mau Baron Starter Story: ad-creative-aligned first 3 screens 3x organic conversion. Adapty 2026 onboarding paywall converts 1.35% (highest of any placement) when the opening anchor primes value. PMC12956955: initial complexity persists across three replications and predicts long-term utility judgment.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) First screen must show CARE, not CAPABILITY: the opening anchor should be an emotional/empathetic framing ('We get it. Quitting smoking is hard.'), not a feature inventory ('AI-powered, with 12 modules'). (2) Mirror the ad creative semantically: pass ad-id as deep-link param, route to onboarding variant whose first 3 screens use the same hook, color, and headline as the ad the user clicked. (3) Cap first-screen visible elements at ≤5 (single headline, single sub-headline, single primary CTA, single skip, optional motif/illustration). (4) Pre-attentive cleanliness: use 60fps fade-in (250-400ms) on screen mount; never render with FOUC or layout shift — first impression is locked in <500ms. (5) Forbid the word 'Welcome' as the headline (default AI-slop opener) — use a problem-statement or promise headline tied to the user's goal. (6) Use Reanimated 3 entrance with damping:18, stiffness:120 spring on hero text; sensoryFeedback .selection on first tap. (7) Architecture: cold-start should hit the first screen within 1.5s on iPhone SE / Pixel 6a; preload assets, use SDUI to skip splash. (8) Pre-warm fonts (Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope) so the first paint uses brand type, not iOS system fallback flash.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI first onboarding screen: a single full-bleed photo of a meal with the tag 'Tracking your meals shouldn't feel like work' — emotional anchor before any feature. (2) Headway first onboarding screen: 'You don't need more time, you need the right ideas' — aspiration-as-anchor, not 'Welcome to Headway'. (3) Headspace post-redesign first screen: 'Here when you need us' — Caregiver archetype anchor. (4) Calm first screen: full-bleed nature footage + ambient audio loop — sensory anchor of calm before the interface. (5) Mau Baron Prayer Lock: opens with affirmation matching the TikTok creative the user came from ('Feel God closer in 30 seconds a day'). (6) Anti-example: a typical AI-wrapper app opening with an empty chat input + cursor blink — the anchor is 'this is yet another ChatGPT clone', and conversion craters. (7) Anti-example: 'Welcome! Sign up to continue' as the first screen — Premature Authentication antipattern AND a capability-not-care anchor.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile is far more anchor-sensitive: full-bleed first screen, push-onto-stack motion, haptic+visual+audio triple-channel (sensoryFeedback .selection on first tap, brand audio sting under 200ms), and the user just made the active decision to install — the anchor lands deeper than a web visit. Web first impressions form on visual aesthetic alone (Lindgaard 50ms). Mobile-primary; web equivalent: fold-1 hero with single CTA + emotional headline, no nav clutter.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Strongly aligned with 2025-2026 platform languages. Apple Liquid Glass (WWDC25) bolder left-aligned typography for alerts and onboarding makes the emotional anchor headline feel native; translucent material and dynamic morph let the first-screen entrance be visceral without violating system feel. Material 3 Expressive (I/O 2025, 18k-participant study) confirms expressive shape + motion accelerate element recognition 4x — meaning a well-designed expressive first screen lands faster as an anchor. Both platforms reward the 'care anchor' move because they removed legacy Welcome-screen patterns from their HIG.
fields.category_fit: Universal — every category benefits. Critical for: AI-wrapper (the anchor is what differentiates from the indigo-slop default), wellness/habit (Cal AI / Flo / Prayer Lock all win on opening anchor), dating (first impression is core product metaphor), edtech (Headway, Blinkist). Less critical: pure-utility (calculator, flashlight) where users skip past the anchor anyway. DO NOT open with a feature carousel, an account form, or the word 'Welcome' for any consumer category in 2026.
fields.tooling_options: Plotline (deep-link variant routing for ad-creative alignment), Adapty (server-driven first-screen A/B), AppsFlyer / Branch / Adjust (deep-link param passing for ad-id), PostHog (session recording on first 7s), UXCam (first-screen heatmap + drop-off), Mobbin (competitive teardown reference for opening screen patterns), Reanimated 3 + Skia (entrance animation).
fields.ai_dependency: none: first-impression anchor is a static design + microcopy + motion problem; no LLM call required. Optional: small-model classifier in CI to grade onboarding screen 1 against an anchor rubric (care vs capability, element count, archetype voice).
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude to engineer the opening anchor explicitly. Example fragment 1: 'Build the first onboarding screen as the emotional anchor. Hard rules: (a) headline is a problem-statement or promise tied to the user goal, NOT "Welcome", NOT a feature inventory, max 9 words, 36-48pt Cabinet Grotesk Bold left-aligned (Liquid Glass alignment); (b) ≤5 visible interactive elements (headline, sub-headline, primary CTA, skip, motif); (c) entrance animation: 350ms spring (damping:18, stiffness:120) staggered on headline → sub → CTA; (d) sensoryFeedback .selection on first tap; (e) preload Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope to avoid system-font flash; (f) no logo lock-up — the anchor must be the headline, not the brand mark.' Example fragment 2: 'Audit the first 3 onboarding screens for ad-creative alignment: list the ad creative\'s color, headline, and emotional register, and verify each of screens 1-3 reuses at least 2 of the 3. Output the alignment table as a comment block above the component.'
fields.stitch_application: Stitch must be told the opening is the anchor, not a welcome. Example fragment 1: 'Mobile onboarding screen 1 of 8, dimensions 390x844. Full-bleed warm cinematic photograph (NOT illustration, NOT abstract gradient) covering top 55% of viewport. Bottom 45% is a soft gradient overlay reading the brand color #FF6B47 to transparent. Headline at 44pt Cabinet Grotesk Bold left-aligned, 32dp left padding, 8 words max, problem-statement register (e.g. \'Quitting smoking is harder than it should be\'). Sub-headline at 16pt Manrope 400, 2 lines max, immediately under headline. Single primary CTA pinned bottom thumb zone, 56dp tall, full-width-minus-32dp, brand color background, label \'I\'m ready\'. Top-right text-only Skip in 14pt Manrope. Forbid the word \'Welcome\'. Forbid feature inventory. Forbid sign-up form on this screen.' Example fragment 2: 'Generate 3 first-screen variants matching 3 distinct ad creatives (warm-empathetic, identity-affirming, transformation-magic). Each variant must reuse the ad\'s primary color, primary motif (e.g. flame, sun, mountain), and emotional register.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen defaults to an opener that is exactly the wrong anchor: (1) the word 'Welcome to [App Name]' as headline; (2) a feature carousel with 4 dots and swipe affordance; (3) a stock indigo-500 → purple-blue gradient background with a Lucide sparkle icon; (4) a sign-up form on screen 1 (Premature Authentication antipattern); (5) Inter as the type. Each of these reads as AI-slop and locks a 'generic capability tool' anchor. Counter-prompt: 'The first screen is the anchor, not a welcome. Forbid the word "Welcome". Forbid feature carousel. Forbid Inter (use Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope). Forbid indigo-500 / purple-blue gradient — use brand palette explicitly listed at top of file. Forbid Lucide sparkle icon. Require: emotional problem-statement headline, ≤5 visible elements, 350ms entrance spring, brand-type pre-warmed before first paint. Output a justification comment block: which emotion does this screen anchor, and which words/visuals carry it?'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) 'Welcome!' headline (the default lazy opener) — anchors the experience as generic. (2) Feature carousel as screen 1 — capability anchor instead of care anchor; deck-of-cards Wall-of-Text antipattern. (3) Sign-up wall as screen 1 — Premature Authentication; locks 'this app is just another walled garden' anchor. (4) Generic stock illustration / indigo-purple gradient — AI-slop anchor. (5) System font flash (FOUC) on first paint — the anchor lands wrong because brand type appears 200ms too late. (6) Logo-prominent splash with no value statement — the anchor is the brand mark, but users haven't earned context for it. (7) Permission cold-ask on screen 1 (Contextless Permissions). (8) Cookie/ATT prompt before the first emotional anchor — kills the anchor entirely.
fields.regulatory_risk: Low: the anchoring tactic itself is permissible; risk arises only when used to bury legal disclosures. EU Digital Fairness Act (Q4 2026 draft) and DSA dark-pattern rules require informed consent visibility — do NOT use 'care anchor' framing to obscure auto-renewal terms. Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization in onboarding constrains first-screen data asks. COPPA (2026-04-22 deadline) requires neutral birthdate gates positioned without pressure framing.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) bolder left-aligned alert typography normalizes the headline-as-anchor pattern. Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization in onboarding — first screen cannot ask for non-essential data. Apple Guideline 4.8 sign-in alternatives — if first screen does require auth, must offer alternatives. Google Play Data Safety + target API 35 (2025-08-31) — runtime permissions cannot be cold-asked on first screen. ATT cold-ask without value framing on screen 1 will tank opt-in to ~25% baseline.
fields.accessibility_implications: First-screen entrance animation must respect prefers-reduced-motion (replace with crossfade ≤200ms). Headline must be VoiceOver/TalkBack readable as the first focus target — not buried beneath a decorative motif. WCAG 2.2 §1.4.3 contrast 4.5:1 must hold against any background photo (use overlay scrim). Touch target on primary CTA ≥44×44 pt iOS / 48dp Android (WCAG 2.2 §2.5.8 also requires ≥24×24 px). Dynamic type / text-scaling to 200% must not break the headline layout — avoid fixed-height headers on first screen. Audio sting on first paint must respect mute switch.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Anchor mismatch with paid acquisition creative — if ad shows a fitness coach and screen 1 shows abstract gradients, the anchor is broken and conversion drops. (2) Over-engineered anchor (4-second cinematic intro) becomes friction; cap entrance at 800ms. (3) Locale calibration — emotional register that lands warmly in US/LATAM reads as oversharing in JP/KR. (4) Cold-start performance: a beautifully designed anchor that takes 4s to render is worse than a plain anchor that paints in 800ms — first-impression latency is part of the anchor. (5) Anchor decay across A/B tests — shifting the first screen is the highest-leverage A/B test, but also the riskiest if winner regresses on a different cohort. (6) Authentic UGC anchor (a real customer face) outperforms stock photography — but requires creative pipeline.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Anchoring bias itself is universal (replicated in 30+ countries; Furnham & Boo 2011 review). Cultural calibration applies to the *register* of the anchor: Western markets (US, UK, AU) tolerate direct emotional appeals ('Quitting is hard'); JP/KR prefer indirect, restrained register ('A small step, every day'); LATAM rewards warmth and family-framing; DACH (DE/AT/CH) wants competence-anchored proof rather than emotion. RTL (AR, HE) requires mirrored layout AND the headline-as-anchor pattern still holds. Localize anchor copy + visual register, not just translate.
fields.prescribed_action: Engineer the first onboarding screen as the emotional anchor: a problem-statement or promise headline (≤9 words, no 'Welcome', no feature inventory), ≤5 visible elements, 350ms entrance spring, sensoryFeedback on first tap, brand type pre-warmed before first paint, and semantic alignment with the ad creative the user clicked. Audit the first 3 screens for ad-creative-color/motif/register continuity and reject any that opens with a sign-up form, indigo-purple gradient, or 'Welcome'.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: The first questionnaire screen is the strongest anchor — sequence the lowest-friction, most identity-affirming question first ('What brings you here today?' as a 4-option big-button), never a demographic data ask. The first question's tone locks the entire questionnaire's perceived register; if it feels like data extraction, every subsequent question feels heavier (load + anchor compound).
fields.aha_relevance: First-impression anchor is what makes the engineered Aha (AI plan reveal) land: a user anchored on 'this app cares about my problem' will *feel* the AI plan reveal as a payoff; a user anchored on 'this app is harvesting my data' will feel it as creepy. The anchor sets the emotional context the Aha is interpreted within.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Universal — first-screen anchor must match the chosen archetype voice. Caregiver (gentle empathetic anchor — 'Here when you need us'), Magician (transformation promise — 'A new chapter starts here'), Jester (playful subversion — 'No more boring fitness apps'), Outlaw (rule-breaking framing — 'Quit the rules. Start over.'). Pairs with .agent/research/emotional-design/results/Jungian_Brand_Archetypes.json. Conflicts with archetype-mismatched defaults (e.g. Caregiver app opening with Hero-aggressive copy 'Crush your goals' anchors wrongly).
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Wall-of-Text + Cognitive Overload (deck-of-cards welcome carousel); Premature Authentication (sign-up wall on screen 1); Generic Celebration (mismatched archetype voice); AI-slop opener (Inter + indigo-500 + Lucide sparkle).
sources: [NN/g — First Impressions Matter: 50ms to a Good First Impression](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/first-impressions-human-automaticity/)
[Furnham & Boo (2011) — Literature Review of the Anchoring Effect](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1053535711000564)
